bibliographic_data.title: Flexible Control Flow Graph Alignment for Delivering Data-Driven Feedback to Novice Programming Learners
bibliographic_data.abstract: Supporting learners in introductory programming assignments at scale is a necessity. This support includes automated feedback on what learners did incorrectly. Existing approaches cast the problem as automatically repairing learners’ incorrect programs extrapolating the data from an existing correct program from other learners. However, such approaches are limited because they only compare programs with similar control flow and order of statements. A potentially valuable set of repair feedback from flexible comparisons is thus missing. In this paper, we present several modifications to CLARA, a data-driven automated repair approach that is open source, to deal with real-world introductory programs. We extend CLARA’s abstract syntax tree processor to handle common introductory programming constructs. Additionally, we propose a flexible alignment algorithm over control flow graphs where we enrich nodes with semantic annotations extracted from programs using operations and calls. Using this alignment, we modify an incorrect program’s control flow graph to match correct programs to apply CLARA’s original repair process. We evaluate our approach against a baseline on the twenty most popular programming problems in Codeforces. Our results indicate that flexible alignment has a significantly higher percentage of successful repairs at 46% compared to 5% for baseline CLARA.

core_content.novelty_claim: The paper presents modifications to the CLARA approach, including a flexible alignment algorithm over control flow graphs that enriches nodes with semantic annotations and modifies incorrect programs to match correct ones.
core_content.key_methodology: The method involves extending CLARA’s abstract syntax tree processor to handle common introductory programming constructs and proposing a flexible alignment algorithm that uses control flow graphs enriched with semantic annotations from operations and calls.
outcomes: Successfully aligns incorrect programs with correct ones using a flexible approach, leading to higher repair success rates (46%) compared to the baseline CLARA (5%).
Implemented and evaluated on twenty popular programming problems in Codeforces.
core_content.full_text_markdown: The paper addresses the challenge of delivering automated feedback at scale for novice programming learners. Existing approaches like CLARA focus on rigid comparisons, which limit their effectiveness. The authors propose a flexible alignment algorithm that enriches control flow graphs with semantic annotations and modifies incorrect programs to match correct ones. This approach is evaluated against a baseline on popular Codeforces problems, showing significant improvements in repair success rates.
